heading: Simplified supplemental nutrition assistance program

children.heading: “Federal costs” defined
children.label: a “Federal costs” defined
children.text: In this section, the term “Federal costs” does not include any Federal costs incurred under .section 2026 of this title

children.heading: Election
children.label: b Election
children.text: Subject to subsection (d), a State may elect to carry out a simplified supplemental nutrition assistance program (referred to in this section as a “Program”), statewide or in a political subdivision of the State, in accordance with this section.
a household in which no members receive assistance under a State program funded under part A of title IV of the Social Security Act ( et seq.) may not participate in the Program;42 U.S.C. 601

a household in which all members receive assistance under a State program funded under part A of title IV of the Social Security Act ( et seq.) shall automatically be eligible to participate in the Program;42 U.S.C. 601

if approved by the Secretary, a household in which 1 or more members but not all members receive assistance under a State program funded under part A of title IV of the Social Security Act ( et seq.) may be eligible to participate in the Program; and42 U.S.C. 601

children.label: A
children.text: a State program funded under part A of title IV of the Social Security Act ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 601

children.label: B
children.text: the supplemental nutrition assistance program; or

children.label: C
children.text: a combination of a State program funded under part A of title IV of the Social Security Act ( et seq.) and the supplemental nutrition assistance program.42 U.S.C. 601

children.heading: State plan
children.label: 1 State plan
children.text: A State agency may not operate a Program unless the Secretary approves a State plan for the operation of the Program under paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: complies with this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: contains sufficient documentation that the plan will not increase Federal costs for any fiscal year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall determine whether a Program being carried out by a State agency is increasing Federal costs under this chapter.

children.heading: No excluded households
children.label: B No excluded households
children.text: In making a determination under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall not require the State agency to collect or report any information on households not included in the Program.

children.heading: Alternative accounting periods
children.label: C Alternative accounting periods
children.text: The Secretary may approve the request of a State agency to apply alternative accounting periods to determine if Federal costs do not exceed the Federal costs had the State agency not elected to carry out the Program.

children.heading: Notification
children.label: 2 Notification
children.text: If the Secretary determines that the Program has increased Federal costs under this chapter for any fiscal year or any portion of any fiscal year, the Secretary shall notify the State not later than 30 days after the Secretary makes the determination under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Corrective action
children.label: A Corrective action
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the date of a notification under paragraph (2), the State shall submit a plan for approval by the Secretary for prompt corrective action that is designed to prevent the Program from increasing Federal costs under this chapter.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: B Termination
children.text: If the State does not submit a plan under subparagraph (A) or carry out a plan approved by the Secretary, the Secretary shall terminate the approval of the State agency operating the Program and the State agency shall be ineligible to operate a future Program.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In operating a Program, a State or political subdivision of a State may follow the rules and procedures established by the State or political subdivision under a State program funded under part A of title IV of the Social Security Act ( et seq.) or under the supplemental nutrition assistance program.42 U.S.C. 601

children.heading: Standardized deductions
children.label: 2 Standardized deductions
children.text: In operating a Program, a State or political subdivision of a State may standardize the deductions provided under . In developing the standardized deduction, the State shall consider the work expenses, dependent care costs, and shelter costs of participating households.section 2014(e) of this title

children.label: G

children.heading: Limitation on eligibility
children.label: 4 Limitation on eligibility
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, a household may not receive benefits under this section as a result of the eligibility of the household under a State program funded under part A of title IV of the Social Security Act ( et seq.), unless the Secretary determines that any household with income above 130 percent of the poverty guidelines is not eligible for the program.42 U.S.C. 601
